Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Provenzano Bros., Inc., And Railway Builders Supply, Inc.
Off Calendar Feb-08-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Provenzano Brothers, Inc. and Railway Builders Supply, Inc.
Dept. 514. Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Nichole M. Rodich, CSR #11604. Telephone: 415-551-0616.

Michael Espinosa, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Margaret Siefken, as Wrongful Death Heir, and as Successor-in-Interest to Milton Siefken, Deceased, and Larry Siefken, as Legal Heir of Milton Siefken, Deceased.
No appearance by defaulted defendants Provenzano Brothers, Inc. or Railway Builders Supply, Inc.

Default was entered against defendant Provenzano Brothers, Inc. on March 11, 2013.
Statement of Damages as to defendant Provenzano Brothers, Inc. was filed and served on March 11, 2013.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on January 8, 2018.

Default was entered against defendant Railway Builders Supply, Inc. on February 11, 2015.
Statement of Damages as to defendant Railway Builders Supply, Inc. was filed and served on February 11, 2015.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on January 8, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Margaret Siefken, as Wrongful Death Heir, and as Successor-in-Interest to Milton Siefken, Deceased, and Larry Siefken, as Legal Heir of Milton Siefken, Deceased, and against the Defaulted Defendants Provenzano Brothers, Inc., and Railway Builders Supply, Inc., as follows:

Defendant Provenzano Brothers, Inc:
Economic Damages: $568,415.28.
Non-Economic Damages - $11,000,000.00.
Total - $11,568,415.28.

Defendant John H. Broad & Associates, Inc:
Economic Damages: $568,415.28.
Non-Economic Damages - $11,000,000.00.
Total - $11,568,415.28.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Joseph R. Connelly III of HAWKINS PARNELL THACKSTON & YOUNG (415-766-3208) appearing for defendant/ moving party Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.

RULING:
DEFENDANT KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

No objection made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.'s general demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Plaintiffs did not substantively oppose defendant's demurrer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
